evals.prompt: generate the API for this RFC: requirements/rfc-review/scripts/fixtures/well_structured_rfc.md
evals.expected_output: api.ir.json plus openapi.json and/or schema.graphql and API_NOTES.md written next to the RFC and shown inline. Since the RFC describes rate-limit response behavior (HTTP 429 with Retry-After) rather than new CRUD operations, the skill either models it as an explicit operation/error case or says plainly that no concrete API surface could be derived, rather than inventing endpoints.
assertions: api.ir.json, at least one rendered target, and API_NOTES.md are all written next to the RFC and shown inline
No auth scopes/roles are invented since the RFC never states any
If operations can't be concretely identified, the skill says so rather than fabricating an API surface

evals.prompt: give me the OpenAPI spec for this proposal: requirements/rfc-review/scripts/fixtures/adr_style.md
evals.expected_output: Because this ADR describes a storage decision, not API behavior, the skill either derives a thin plausible API for the described entity or explains clearly why there isn't enough API-shaped detail to derive one — never inventing an API surface with no grounding.
assertions: The skill does not invent operations the source document gives no basis for
If it does propose operations, every operation's kind is one of create/read/list/update/delete/action, not an HTTP verb
